agents.name: Summarization Agent
agents.description: Produces structured natural-language summaries of ITSM records (incidents, requests, problems, changes) plus asset and CMDB CI contexts when applicable. Routing often aligns with conversational phrases such as 'summarize this ticket'; the canonical agent is selected by the planner using ticket id prefixes, UI context, and registry-grounded rules. For each (agent_id, service_id) pair, the executor's allowlisted tools and their invocation order are defined in agent-tool-mapping.json—typically: optional AI cache read, core ticket fields, timeline, cross-links, attachment metadata (names and ids only), synthesis, then cache write. Every tool in this agent's surface is read-only toward ITSM: they fetch data and manage an AI-side summary cache; they do not mutate ticket bodies or workflow state. Plan steps must pass a consistent service_id in parameters so parameterized tool implementations resolve the correct module.

agents.name: Similar Tickets Agent
agents.description: Surfaces semantically and metadata-aligned peer tickets for pattern discovery, duplicate awareness, and resolution reuse across incidents, requests, problems, and changes. Work is driven by the allowlisted find_similar_entities tool family (scoped by service_id in parameters). Typical inputs include ticket_id, tenant_id, and service_id; optional filters such as time window, status preference, similarity thresholds, and scope flags are defined on the tool entries in tool-registry.json. Results must respect tenant isolation and visibility—callers should not see candidates they cannot access in the source system.

agents.name: Knowledge Base Lookup Agent
agents.description: Retrieves ranked knowledge articles for chat questions, ticket-in-context scenarios, and explicit article opens. Three complementary paths are modeled: natural-language query search, ticket-grounded search (title, description, category signals), and fetch-by-id for a known article. Planner and executor bind to search_kb, search_kb_by_ticket, and get_kb_article as listed in agent-tool-mapping.json. Parameters usually include tenant_id and one of query, ticket_id, or kb_id, plus optional language, audience, publication filters, category constraints, and result limits as specified per tool in tool-registry.json.

agents.name: Sentiment Analysis Agent
agents.description: Infers end-user sentiment (e.g. frustration, satisfaction, neutrality) from ticket narrative and, when provided, conversation thread context—suited to agent-facing badges, escalation hints, and quality analytics. Scope is incidents and requests only (read path). Execution centers on analyze_entity_sentiment; inputs routinely include ticket_id, tenant_id, and service_id. Optional controls include analysis_scope (ticket-wide vs single comment vs full thread trajectory), comment identifiers or text for triggered analysis, caps on customer comments for long threads, locale or language hints, and whether to include non-customer-authored content, as documented on the tool definition.

agents.name: Resolution Suggestion Agent
agents.description: Proposes ranked resolution hypotheses from similar resolved work, knowledge, and known-error signals, then optionally records whether the user found a given suggestion helpful. suggest_resolution is a read step; record_feedback is an action that stores structured feedback for future ranking. Both require service_id incident or request. Read path parameters often include filters to exclude steps already reflected in work notes, toggles for which evidence sources to include, a lookback window for precedent search, and formatting suitable for chat versus structured UI. Feedback payloads identify the suggestion target type, rank position (e.g. 1–5), and a helpful or not-helpful outcome so downstream models and analytics can learn without blocking the main resolution flow.

agents.name: Triage Agent
agents.description: Automates classification, priority assessment, near-duplicate detection against recent work, and assignment recommendations for incidents and requests. The ordered tool set (classify_entity, prioritize_entity, check_duplicate_candidates, assign_entity) and per-service rows live in agent-tool-mapping.json. Supports both advisory mode—returning recommendations without persisting—and explicit application of assignment when policy and user consent allow. service_id must be incident or request; mixed read and action operation types reflect pre-check tools before mutating assignment state.

agents.name: Ticket Action Agent
agents.description: Performs state-changing and communicative work on existing incidents and requests: lifecycle transitions (close, reopen, resolve, cancel), threaded comments (customer-visible or internal work notes), sparse field patches that avoid implicit state jumps (priority, title, description, category, urgency, and similar scalar or small-struct updates), and relationship edges including duplicate, related, parent, and child links with optional cross-service targeting via target_service_id. Entirely action-typed toward ITSM; every invocation must carry a consistent service_id (incident or request) and respect RBAC and approval policy in the integrating platform.

agents.name: Ticket Creation Agent
agents.description: Creates new incidents or service requests through guided slot capture while reducing duplicate noise: a read-style duplicate or open-ticket check typically precedes create_ticket, in the order defined in agent-tool-mapping.json. Supports human-in-the-loop flows via preview-only drafts and explicit user confirmation before submit, on-behalf creation using affected_user_id, and resumption of in-progress forms through a partial draft identifier. service_id distinguishes incident versus request creation; tool parameters for mandatory slots and optional enrichments are authoritative in tool-registry.json.

agents.name: Fulfillment Agent
agents.description: Orchestrates catalog-driven and onboarding-style fulfillment: deduplication checks against in-flight work, status and task-tree inspection, workflow execution or simulation, and granular task updates when technicians complete manual steps. Applies to service_id values catalog, request, or onboarding according to the mapping rows in agent-tool-mapping.json. Flags commonly distinguish plan-only decomposition from execution, template or approval gates, and SLA or exception paths. update_fulfillment_task marks specific fulfillment tasks complete (e.g. technician checklist items), which may unblock dependent tasks or downstream automation in the fulfillment engine.
